task:
ATOM 0 — Owner/objective map first (DOC-ONLY, no code edits). Extend the EXISTING file docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md into the full orchestration-pipeline owner map. Do NOT create a second owner-map doc — a parallel doc is exactly the duplication anti-pattern this priority condemns; everything is added inside this one file.

WHAT TO READ FIRST (required inputs — read before writing):
- ARCHITECTURE.md
- docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md (the file you are extending — its existing Owner Inventory table and the Run_163 closeout-delivery addendum are ALREADY-OWNED rows; fold them in by reference, do not re-derive or duplicate them)
- cocoder/decisions/0010-taxonomy-and-authoring.md, 0012-living-base-personas.md, 0013-orchestration-observation.md, 0023-workspace-commit-spine.md, 0026-onboard-existing-as-oscar-priority.md
- packages/core/src/runner/ , packages/core/src/plays/
- packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts , packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts
- packages/personas/base/
- cocoder/tickets/open/ , cocoder/tickets/closed/0008-post-wrap-founder-interaction-contract.md
- cocoder/priorities/archive/founder-brief-format-durability.md (predecessor priority whose shipped owner map this run absorbs)
- scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs and packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts (existing enforcers — note what they already pin)

WHAT TO PRODUCE (append clearly-headed sections to docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md, under a new top-level section e.g. '## Run_164 — Full Pipeline Owner Map'):
Add FOUR new tables. For every row, cite source-of-truth file:line, every live emitter/consumer file:line, the allowed write path, and the test/proof that pins it (or 'UNPINNED' if none).

1. OBJECTIVES table — CoCoder's orchestration objectives and where each is owned/expressed.
2. COMPONENTS table — the taxonomy of responsibilities from the priority: Work targets (priority, ticket, ad-hoc support, mandatory lifecycle Play); Instruction sources (base personas, shared standards, workspace deltas, Play bodies, generated runner prompts); Execution paths (Oscar directive loop, optional Play request, mandatory Play trigger, ticket-fix launch, drift/onboarding library engines, support-commit, Deb repair); Governance artifacts (priorities, tickets, ADRs/decisions, base personas/Plays/standards, workspace deltas, docs/status surfaces); Commit/verification paths (per-atom verify gate, Deb direct repair, post-wrap support commit, audit write-boundary).
3. LIVE PATHWAYS table — every current orchestration path that can CREATE, MODIFY, LAUNCH, VERIFY, COMMIT, CLOSE, or REPORT work for priorities, tickets, Plays, and base orchestration instructions. One row per (action x path).
4. DUPLICATED/OVERLAPPING PATHS table — every place two or more paths do a similar job. For each, give a PROPOSED disposition (retire / merge / derive-from-owner / guarded-distinction) with one-line rationale and the files/owners/tests affected. Seed from the priority's Known Suspect Overlaps list and confirm or refute each against the code.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- The four commit/landing paths (verify-gate, Deb repair, post-wrap support-commit, audit-apply) are ADR-accepted DISTINCT jobs already examined in this same doc — INGEST that prior disposition; do NOT mark them as duplication to retire. An Accepted-ADR distinction is guarded-by-default.
- ADR-0023 (commit spine) and ADR-0026 (reframe) are in scope to MAP, not reverse.
- DOC-ONLY this atom: no edits to code, tests, scripts, personas, or Plays. Write scope is docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md (and ARCHITECTURE.md ONLY if a factual cross-reference is needed — prefer not).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (judgment-gated, one-shot atom — NOT loop-amenable):
- All four tables present, appended into the existing doc (no new doc file created).
- Existing Owner Inventory rows + Run_163 addendum folded in by reference, not duplicated.
- Every Known Suspect Overlap from the priority is addressed (confirmed or refuted with evidence) in the duplicated-paths table.
- Every claim cites a real file:line. No disposition is left blank.
- The four commit/landing paths are mapped as guarded distinctions, not flagged for retirement.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: no behavior changes; this is analysis only. Do not touch the enforcer script or any test in this atom.

ATOM 1 — Derive priority-markdown from one owner + land the required duplicate-path detector. This is the run's mandatory real simplification (Verified-when #6) plus the mandatory detector (#5), done together because both serve one concern: ONE owner for priority-markdown composition. The atom-0 map identified this as the highest-confidence duplicate — tickets already derive their format from a shared core helper consumed by BOTH the daemon route and the create-ticket Play, but priorities have a LOCAL composer in the daemon route and a create-priority Play that restates the format in prose (a second owner).

FIRST READ (the proven ticket pattern to mirror exactly, and the current priority duplication):
- packages/core/src/tickets/compose.ts (composeTicketMarkdown — the owner-helper shape to mirror)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (how the ticket helper is exported from core)
- packages/daemon/src/routes.ts (createPriority ~254-266 and ~576-603: the LOCAL priority composer to retire; and the ticket route ~611-650 that already derives from composeTicketMarkdown — copy that shape)
- packages/personas/base/plays/create-priority.md (~35-48: prose that restates the priority format — to be replaced by a reference to the helper, mirroring create-ticket.md ~42-56)
- packages/personas/base/plays/create-ticket.md (the reference pattern to mirror)
- packages/core/src/priorities/loader.ts (loadPriority/readPriorities — the round-trip the helper output MUST satisfy)
- packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts and scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs (the EXISTING enforcers to EXTEND — do NOT create a parallel enforcer; see how they pin the closeout/ticket single-owner)

WHAT TO BUILD (order: owner/schema -> rewire consumers -> proof last):
1. Create packages/core/src/priorities/compose.ts exporting composePriorityMarkdown(...) that produces priority markdown byte/round-trip equivalent to what the daemon route currently emits, so every existing cocoder/priorities/*.md still parses unchanged through loadPriority. Mirror composeTicketMarkdown's signature/style.
2. Export composePriorityMarkdown from packages/core/src/index.ts alongside the ticket helper.
3. Rewire daemon createPriority to call composePriorityMarkdown instead of inlining frontmatter/body assembly. No local priority-markdown composer may remain in routes.ts. Keep the existing commitGovernance landing path unchanged.
4. Update create-priority.md to reference composePriorityMarkdown and validate with loadPriority/readPriorities (mirror create-ticket.md), removing the prose restatement of the markdown format. Do NOT change the founder-approved Objective authoring lane or governance authority — only where the FORMAT is owned.
5. EXTEND the existing enforcer (packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts, and the red->green harness scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs) to fail if priority-markdown gets a second owner — e.g. assert the daemon priority route does not inline frontmatter composition and that create-priority Play names the helper, exactly the way the existing checks pin the ticket/closeout single-owner. Demonstrate red->green in the proof harness (enforcer fails against a synthetic duplicate, passes against the real tree).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (judgment-gated, one-shot, NOT loop-amenable):
- composePriorityMarkdown exists in core, is exported, and its output round-trips through loadPriority (existing priority files load unchanged — prove with the priorities loader test).
- Daemon priority route derives from the helper; the inline composer is gone (grep shows no second frontmatter assembly for priorities in routes.ts).
- create-priority.md references the helper and no longer restates the format in prose.
- The detector EXTENDS the existing enforcer/harness (NOT a new parallel file) and fails on a synthetic second priority-markdown owner, passes on the real tree.
- Green: pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test  AND  pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test  AND  node scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs  all pass. Report exact commands + results.

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF BOUNDS: do not change the priority file format semantics (round-trip equivalent only); do not touch the runner, commit spine, UI, or daemon launch/lifecycle; do not reverse any ADR; do not create a parallel enforcer; do not alter ticket authoring. Keep the diff narrow and mirror the ticket pattern.

ATOM 2 — Closeout decision (the run's FINAL atom; docs + governance only, NO src/test code changes). This satisfies the Objective's atom 5 and Verified-when #6's clause that every remaining overlap must exit as a guarded distinction or a NAMED follow-up — never an unowned intention. The two hard requirements already shipped: priority-markdown derived to one owner composePriorityMarkdown (atom 1, commit 198ae88) and the duplicate-path detector extended in the existing enforcer (priority RED→GREEN). Atom 0 (commit 73e311c) produced the full owner map in docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md.

FIRST READ: docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md (the Run_164 owner map + its Duplicated/Overlapping Paths table), cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md, packages/core/src/tickets/compose.ts and packages/core/src/tickets/loader.ts (composeTicketMarkdown/loadTicket — the format your new tickets MUST round-trip through), an existing open ticket e.g. cocoder/tickets/open/0020-stale-governance-test-archived-hybrid-plays.md (mirror its frontmatter/shape), cocoder/failure-catalog.md (format to append to).

WHAT TO PRODUCE:
1. Append a '## Run_164 Closeout' section to docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md containing:
   a. WHAT WAS SIMPLIFIED THIS RUN: priority-markdown derived to single owner composePriorityMarkdown in packages/core/src/priorities/compose.ts (atom 1, commit 198ae88); the daemon route's inline composer retired; create-priority.md now references the helper; the detector extended in packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts + scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs (priority RED→GREEN). Atom 0 owner map shipped in commit 73e311c.
   b. A DISPOSITION TABLE with one row for EVERY overlap in the Run_164 'Duplicated / Overlapping Paths' table. Each row's disposition MUST be exactly one of: GUARDED-BY-ADR (name the ADR, e.g. ADR-0010/0012/0023/0026), LANDED-GUARD (name the enforcer test that pins it), or NAMED-FOLLOW-UP (name the ticket/priority file id). No row may be left as an intention without an owner. The four commit/landing paths and other Accepted-ADR distinctions are GUARDED-BY-ADR by default — do not propose retiring them.
   c. THE SINGLE NEXT LAUNCHABLE REFACTOR ATOM: name the highest-value remaining derive/retire (one concrete next step), so a fresh session can launch it.
2. Create well-formed follow-up tickets in cocoder/tickets/open/ (each MUST round-trip through loadTicket — use the composeTicketMarkdown frontmatter shape; add an INDEX.md row for each) for the genuinely-unowned remaining items discovered during this run:
   - A ticket for the PRE-EXISTING daemon typecheck failures: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec tsc --noEmit` fails in three untouched test-mock files — tests/play-delta-launch.test.ts (input.wrapPlay possibly undefined), tests/read-surfaces.test.ts (Git mock missing hasUpstream/push), tests/static.test.ts (SessionHost mock mismatch). Unrelated to this priority; discovered during atom-1 verify.
   - A ticket for the PROCESS GAP: the daemon test suite was RED on main before run_164 because packages/daemon/tests/helpers/founder-closeout.ts drifted from the wrap-up Play closeout contract; a prior wrap-up Play contract change shipped without running the daemon suite. Atom 1 repaired the helper (now derives from the Play contract), but the ticket should propose pinning that the daemon suite runs on wrap-up Play contract changes so this class cannot recur silently.
   - For any overlap in the disposition table you classify as NAMED-FOLLOW-UP that does not already have a ticket/priority file, create that file too (small and concrete; do not over-create).
3. Append one entry to cocoder/failure-catalog.md for the stale-test-helper-vs-owner-contract class (daemon suite silently red because a hand-maintained test fixture re-encoded an owner contract and drifted), with the fix pattern: derive fixtures from the owner contract, and run the consuming package's suite when the owner contract changes.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (judgment-gated, one-shot, NOT loop-amenable):
- docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md has the Run_164 Closeout section with a disposition for EVERY overlap row (explicitly none left as an unowned intention), and names the single next launchable atom.
- Each new ticket round-trips through loadTicket and has an INDEX.md row (prove by reading them back / running the tickets test).
- failure-catalog.md has the new class entry.
- NO changes to src or test code — governance/docs only. Confirm with `git diff --stat` that only docs/**, cocoder/tickets/**, cocoder/failure-catalog.md changed.
- Existing tests remain green: run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (must stay 439/439) and report the result.

MUST NOT: change any src or test file; reverse any ADR; propose retiring an Accepted-ADR distinction; create a parallel owner-map doc (extend the existing one); over-create tickets.
